solutions.solution: According to the diagram, number 8 lies only inside the triangle.
Number common to circle and triangle = 4, Number common to rectangle and triangle = 9, Number common to circle and rectangle = 2
According to the diagram, Common number that lies inside all figures = 5
Statement given in option (d) is correct because only B is common in all three figures.
The region 4 is common to youth and job but not educated.
The region which represents students who study Biology and computer but not Mathematics is '7'.
There are 25 players who are neither artists nor doctors because this is the only region of the circle which is not common with either rectangle or triangle.
Required number = 32 + 3 = 25
There are 30 artists who are neither doctors nor players because this is the only region of the rectangle which is not common with either circle or triangle.
There are 17 doctors who are neither players nor artists because this is the only region of the triangle which is not common with either circle or rectangle.
The region 3 is common to triangle, circle and rectangle and hence, represents doctors who are players as well as artists.
The part of figure representing officers, who are graduates and post-graduates is common to all three circles i.e., G.
Region T is common to the both singers and poets but not dancers.
According to the diagram, regions b and d represent appointed educated persons whose job is not permanent.
Letter e represents the typists who are only graduates but not Government employees.
Letter g represents the typists who are only Government employees but not graduates.
Statement (c) is true because some portion of rectangle is common to both the triangle and the circle.
The circle and the rectangle have some area in common which indicates that some historians are philosophers.
Because no portion of the figure shows the common space among the three figures.
Letter D represents those people who are doctors and singers but not players as it is common to triangle and rectangle but not circle.
Region 'T' represents the students who study both Physics and Chemistry but not Mathematics.
People who are only studious are represented by V.
O shows the persons who are laborious, enterprising and disciplined.
Q represents the region of enterprising, studious and disciplined people.
Required region is represented by K.
N shows the region common to all the four circles. i.e. the people possessing all the qualities is represented by N.
Educated men who are not urban are represented by the region which is common to both the triangle and the rectangle but not to the square and circle. So, the required region is 11.
A man who is urban as well as a Governments employee is common to the circle, square and rectangle but not to the triangle. So, the required region is 6.
Number 5 represents those poor boys who help in family business but are not educated or employed elsewhere because it occupies the space common to circle and rectangle only.
No number occupies the space which is common only to the figures— circle, square and triangle.
Number 3 is present in the space common to circle, square and rectangle, hence represents educated poor boys who help in family business.
Number '9' depicts the area where the three languages English, Marathi and Urdu are spoken. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.
Number 2 lies in the space which is common to triangle and square and hence represents English and Hindi speaking area.
Number 3 lies in the space, which is common to triangle, square and rectangle but not to the circle and hence represents the required area.
A and E are graduates or teachers but not politicians because A and E do not fall under the big triangular space.
B and C are graduate politicians but not the members of parliament because B and C do not fall under the square.
L and H are the politicians who are neither teachers nor graduates because L and H do not fall under small triangular space and circular space.
F is the member of parliament who is a graduate as well as a teacher because F is common to circle, square and the small triangle.
Only 11 persons are educated males but not urban.
Only 3 persons are urban, who are neither public servants nor educated or males.
Only 4 educated males are there who belong to urban region.
The group of employees e, g and h represents those employees who are not businessmen.
The group of employees a, d, e and h represents persons who are either employees or businessmen but not female members.
Letters b and c represents female members as well as businessmen.
Letters c and d are businessmen but are neither post graduates nor employees.
Only i represents the female members who are neither businessman nor employees.
The required region is the one which is common to the rectangle, circle and square but lies outside the triangle, i.e., 2.
Letter U represent persons having car, motorcycle, cycle but no autorickshaw.
